content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Frances Hong, city Clerk 
City of stockton 
425 North El Dorado 
stockton, CA 
95202 
Dear Ms. Hong: 
February 16, 1988 
Re: 
Combined Campaign Report 
Our File No. A-88-072 
This is to confirm the advice provided to Nora Keating of your 
office on February 10, 1988, regarding the campaign disclosure 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").Y This letter 
constitutes written advice under section 84205. 
section 84205 states: 
The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit 
candidates and committees to file campaign statements 
combining statements and reports required to be filed by this 
title. 
Ms. Keating stated that City Councilmember John B. Nisby will 
be the subject of a recall election to be held on March 22, 1988. 
Mr. Nisby also plans to run in the city's June 7, 1988, primary 
election. 
To comply with the Act's campaign disclosure 
requirements, Mr. Nisby would have to file five disclosure reports 
during the first six months of 1988, one of which will cover only 
a 12-day period (March 6--the day after the closing date of the 
second pre-election statement in connection with the March 22 
election--through March 17--the closing date of the first 
pre-election statement in connection with the June 7 election). 
Th(:refore, we believe it is appropriate to permit Mr. Nisby to 
conbine two of the required statements. 
YGovernment Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
Section 81000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to 
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322,5660 


Frances Hong 
February 16, 1988 
Page 2 
Ms. Keating was advised that Mr. Nisby should file campaign 
disclosure reports on the following schedule: 
Type of statement 
1st Pre-Election 
for March election 
2nd Pre-Election 
for March combined 
with 1st Pre-Election 
for June election 
2nd Pre-Election 
for June election 
Semi-Annual 
Period Covered 
Filing Deadline 
Jan. 1 -
Feb. 6 
February 11 
Feb. 7 - Mar. 5 
March 10 
Mar. 6 -
May 21 
May 26 
May 22 - June 30 
July 31 
If you have any questions regarding the above, please do not 
hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
By: CttA1A[)" Lz;J tVL~u..J 
Carla 
Wardlow 
Political Reform Consultant

sections.question_synthetic: May a candidate subject to both a recall election and a primary election combine required campaign disclosure reports to avoid filing five separate statements within six months, including one covering only a 12-day period?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC permits Mr. Nisby to combine two of the required campaign disclosure statements—specifically the second pre-election statement for the March recall election with the first pre-election statement for the June primary election—to reduce the burden of overlapping disclosure requirements under Government Code section 84205.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks explicitly labeled QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections but contains clear legal question and FPPC response. The question is synthesized from the factual background and advice provided. Filing schedule table provides specific implementation guidance. Letter references prior telephone advice from February 10, 1988 to Nora Keating.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May a candidate subject to both a recall election and a primary election combine required campaign disclosure reports to avoid filing five separate statements within six months, including one covering only a 12-day period?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC permits Mr. Nisby to combine two of the required campaign disclosure statements—specifically the second pre-election statement for the March recall election with the first pre-election statement for the June primary election—to reduce the burden of overlapping disclosure requirements under Government Code section 84205.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming that a Stockton city councilmember facing both a March 1988 recall election and a June 1988 primary election may combine two campaign disclosure reports to streamline compliance with the Political Reform Act's disclosure requirements.
